data.title: Binance launches prediction market; Hong Kong issues first batch of stablecoin issuer licenses.
data.description:
Golden Weekly is a weekly blockchain industry summary column launched by Golden Finance, covering key news, mining information, project updates, technological advancements, and other industry dynamics. This article is the news segment of the weekly summary, giving you a glimpse into the major events in the blockchain industry this week.

Headlines

▌Circle has minted a total of 10.5 billion USDC on Solana in the past month

On April 11th, according to Lookonchain monitoring, Circle minted an additional 250 million USDC. In the past month, Circle has minted a total of 10.5 billion USDC on Solana.

On April 11, Du Guangda, Deputy Director of the Science and Technology Department of the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, stated that the Ministry will focus on manufacturing as the main battlefield and application-driven development as the main line, releasing a batch of high-value "AI+" scenarios, exploring a batch of typical applications, building a batch of distinctive intelligent entities, providing a batch of new intelligent terminals, developing a batch of new standards, cultivating a batch of industrial application talents, and creating a batch of high-quality enterprises, comprehensively promoting the deep integration of artificial intelligence and manufacturing. (Xinhua) On April 12, according to Iran's Fars News Agency, an Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson said that Iran and the United States reached consensus on some issues, but disagreed on two or three important issues, ultimately failing to reach an agreement. (Xinhua) On April 8, TikTok announced it would invest 1 billion euros to build a second data center in Lahti, Finland, marking the company's second 1 billion euro data center investment project in Finland. The statement said the investment is part of TikTok's €12 billion "Project Clover," which aims to process and protect the data of over 200 million European users. TikTok also stated that it is progressing as planned with the Kouvola data center project in Finland, announced last year. (Jiemian News) On April 12, US President Trump retweeted an article on his social media, stating that if Iran refuses to compromise, its trump card might be controlling the Strait of Hormuz through the US Navy to prevent Iranian oil tankers from entering or leaving, thereby striking at the Iranian economy. (CCTV News) The Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) announced on April 10 that the Commissioner of Finance has granted stablecoin issuer licenses to Dianxin Financial Technology Co., Ltd. (a joint venture between Standard Chartered Bank, Animoca Brands, and Hong Kong Telecom) and HSBC Bank Limited under the Stablecoin Ordinance, allowing them to issue stablecoins in Hong Kong. The licenses took effect today. According to the licensees' business plans, they intend to commence operations in the coming months after completing the necessary preparatory work.

▌“Guideline for Risk Management of OpenClaw-like Intelligent Agent Deployment” Officially Released

On April 10th, during the 17th Plenary Session of the China Artificial Intelligence Industry Development Alliance, the Security Governance Committee of the China Artificial Intelligence Industry Development Alliance officially released the “Guideline for Risk Management of OpenClaw-like Intelligent Agent Deployment.” To systematically address the security challenges posed by OpenClaw-like intelligent agents, the guideline focuses on the pain points of risk prevention and control for enterprises during the technology implementation process. It proposes an overall framework and self-inspection standards for secure deployment covering the entire process of deployment, use, and decommissioning. The aim is to promote the advancement of intelligent agents from “deployable” to “secure deployment, standardized use, and continuous governance,” providing practical guidance for enterprises to conduct standardized technology deployment.

▌Comptroller of the Currency: The U.S. Should Not Hand Over Digital Asset Innovation to Other Countries Due to Regulatory Uncertainty

On April 12, the Comptroller of the Currency stated that the U.S. should not hand over digital asset innovation to other countries due to regulatory uncertainty. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessenter emphasized that clarity is key to maintaining domestic markets, businesses, capital, and technology. A clear framework is how we ensure the U.S. maintains its dominance and advantage in financial innovation, in line with Trump's vision of U.S. leadership in the global economy.

Blockchain Applications

▌Animoca Brands will launch the Hong Kong dollar-backed stablecoin HKDAP in phases starting in Q2 this year

Animoca Brands announced on the X platform that Animoca Brands, a joint venture established by Standard Chartered Bank, HKT and Animoca Brands, will launch the regulated Hong Kong dollar-backed stablecoin HKDAP (HKD At Par) in phases starting in the second quarter of this year. Group President Evan Auyang stated that a properly governed Hong Kong dollar-backed stablecoin is crucial to Hong Kong's financial infrastructure.

Animoca Brands announced on the X platform that Animoca Brands, a joint venture established by Standard Chartered Bank, HKT and Animoca Brands, will launch the regulated Hong Kong dollar-backed stablecoin HKDAP (HKD At Par) in phases starting in the second quarter of this year.

Animoca Brands Group President Evan Auyang stated that a properly governed Hong Kong dollar-backed stablecoin is crucial to Hong Kong's financial infrastructure.

▌Animoca Brands will launch the Hong Kong dollar-backed stablecoin HKDAP (HKD At Par) in phases starting in Q2 this year ...strong>

▌Meta Pledges Additional $21 Billion Investment in CoreWeave

On April 9th, according to CNBC: Meta Platforms (META.O) pledged an additional $21 billion investment in CoreWeave.

▌Binance Officially Launches Prediction Market

On April 9th, Binance announced the launch of its prediction market. This new feature allows users to participate in probability-based markets from the Binance app through integration with third-party platforms. Upon launch, the Binance Wallet will integrate market access provided by Predict.fun, a leading on-chain prediction market provider on the BNB Smart Chain (BSC). In the Binance App, go to the [Markets] tab. Click the [Prediction] tab (regions only).

Select a category to browse available marketplaces that offer real-time probability pricing.

    ▌Coinbase Obtains Australian Financial Services License, Plans to Expand Stock Trading and Payments Business

    On April 8th, Coinbase announced that it will expand its business to stock trading and payments in Australia after obtaining a financial services license. The company stated that it has obtained an Australian Financial Services License (AFSL) from the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC), covering retail derivatives authorization, and stated that it is the first crypto exchange to receive such approval directly from the regulator.

      ▌Polygon Foundation: Giugliano Hard Fork to Take Place on Polygon Mainnet on April 8th UTC

      On April 7th, the Polygon Foundation announced that the Giugliano hard fork will be launched on the Polygon mainnet at block height 85,268,500, expected to take place at 2 PM UTC on April 8th. This upgrade includes: faster finality by allowing block producers to broadcast blocks earlier; adding fee parameters directly to the block header; and adding RPC support for fee data.

      All node operators must upgrade Bor to v2.7.0 (or Erigon to v3.5.0).

        Cryptocurrency

        ▌SpaceX still holds $603 million worth of Bitcoin despite a nearly $5 billion loss in 2025

        On April 12th, according to Arkham data and a report released Friday evening by The Information, SpaceX currently holds 8,285 Bitcoins in its Coinbase Prime escrow account, worth approximately $603 million, while the company is projected to lose nearly $5 billion in 2025. This loss is significantly different from the previous year, when SpaceX achieved a profit of approximately $8 billion in 2024, with revenue estimated between $15 billion and $16 billion.

        While SpaceX's revenue is projected to reach $18.5 billion in 2025, the integration costs of xAI, Musk's AI company, into SpaceX in February of this year outweighed the revenue growth. Despite significant losses, SpaceX's Bitcoin holdings have remained unchanged. CoinDesk's analysis of transaction records shows that the most recent major fund movement occurred approximately four months ago, a rebalancing operation involving the transfer of 614 and 1,021 Bitcoins between SpaceX's internal wallets. The holdings have remained stable since mid-2024, having previously exceeded $1.6 billion in market value during Bitcoin's all-time high in October 2025. SpaceX is currently the fourth-largest known company in the world in terms of Bitcoin holdings, after Strategy, Marathon Digital, and Riot Platforms. SpaceX has filed for an IPO, at which time its Bitcoin holdings will appear in public filings for the first time and may be required to undergo fair value accounting under the new FASB guidelines that will take effect at the end of 2025.

        ▌Sources: Gemini Plans to Convert Winklevoss Twins' Approximately $330 Million Loan into Equity

        On April 11, according to Bloomberg, Gemini (GEMI), the cryptocurrency exchange founded by the Winklevoss twins, has seen its market capitalization more than halved this year, with its stock price falling by over 50% cumulatively. The company has already laid off approximately 30% of its staff and withdrawn from major overseas markets such as the UK, EU, and Australia, facing increasing financial pressure. Gemini is internally discussing a plan: requesting the founding twins to forgive or convert approximately $330 million in loans they provided into equity to alleviate its debt burden. These loans were primarily provided through Winklevoss Capital Fund LLC. As of the end of December 2025, Gemini still owed 4,619 bitcoins (worth over $330 million at the time). If this move is realized, as the twins are the main controllers of the company, it will directly impact the company's capital structure.

        Following the report's release, Gemini's stock price initially rose by approximately 9%, but still fell significantly for the year. The company has previously experienced executive departures, a shift in business towards prediction markets, and shareholder lawsuits; this loan focus further highlights the ongoing impact of the crypto winter on exchanges.

        ▌TD Cowen Analyst Lowers MSTR Stock Price Target from $440 to $350

        On April 11th, Lance Vitanza, an analyst at the $15 billion investment bank TD Cowen, lowered the price target for Bitcoin treasury company Strategy ($MSTR) from $440 to $350, but maintained a "buy" rating.

        ...

        ▌Grayscale Releases Latest List of Candidate Assets, Adding HYPE, STX, VIRTUAL, etc.

        According to official news, digital asset investment platform Grayscale recently updated its list of candidate assets for its products, covering six major crypto sectors. The newly added candidate assets are particularly concentrated in the AI ​​and finance categories. The AI ​​sector has the most newly added candidate assets, including Fabric Protocol, Flock, Grass, Kaito, Kite AI, Nous Research, Poseidon, Venice, and Virtuals Protocol, totaling 9 projects. These are considered alongside Bittensor, Livepeer, Near, Render, and Story, reflecting the continued focus of institutions on the AI ​​+ crypto sector.

        In the financial sector, seven DeFi projects—Ethena, Hyperliquid, Jupiter, Kamino Finance, Maple Finance, Morpho, and Pendle—have been added to the shortlist, complementing existing offerings such as Aave, Aerodrome, Curve, Ondo, and Uniswap. The smart contract platform sector has added six projects: Celo, Mantle, MegaETH, Monad, Toncoin, and Tron, with Canton Network also included. The utility tools sector has added DoubleZero, Geodnet, Helium, Jito, LayerZero, and Wormhole. Currently, Grayscale's existing product suite covers major assets including BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, AVAX, BNB, and ADA. The shortlist does not guarantee the launch of related products; Grayscale states that it will update the list within 15 days after the end of each quarter.

        ▌Galaxy Research Director: Whether Iran Uses Bitcoin to Collect Strait of Hormuz Passage Fees Remains Controversial

        On April 11, Alex Thorn, Research Director of Galaxy, published a lengthy analysis on the X platform, stating that reports regarding Iran demanding ships pay Strait of Hormuz passage fees in Bitcoin have not been fully verified. Previously, the Financial Times reported that Iran planned to charge $1 per barrel of oil and require payment in Bitcoin; however, information from various sources differs. Bloomberg reported that the fees might be settled in stablecoins or the yuan, while research firm Citrini Research pointed out that some ships completed payments by unfreezing Iranian overseas assets. Blockchain analytics firm TRM Labs also stated that there is currently no evidence that cryptocurrencies are being used on a large scale for such passage fee payments.

        Alex Thorn believes that if it's true that Bitcoin is used to collect tolls, Bitcoin has certain advantages as a censorship-resistant payment tool. However, the claim that "fast payments enhance privacy" is inaccurate unless layer-two solutions like the Lightning Network are used. Furthermore, considering that a single toll could range from $200,000 to $2 million, large on-chain or Lightning Network payments still face practical limitations. Alex Thorn revealed that Galaxy Research will conduct cross-analysis of ship AIS data and on-chain transactions to determine if there are corresponding-scale Bitcoin transactions. While there are historical precedents of Iran using crypto assets for cross-border transactions, the claim of "Bitcoin collecting tolls" still needs further verification.

        Important Economic Developments

        ▌Polish Central Bank to Continue Gold Purchases

        April 9th ​​news: Polish Central Bank Governor Grapinski stated that the Polish Central Bank will continue to purchase gold.

        Poland reiterated its goal of increasing its gold reserves to 700 tons; the central bank currently holds 580 tons. White House National Economic Council Director Hassett: He expects Powell not to remain on the Federal Reserve Board. He is confident that Warsh will become Federal Reserve Chairman in May. Bloomberg Strategist: Gold's Parabolic Peak May Indicate Risk of US Stock Market Correction Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Commodity Strategist Mike McGlone wrote on the X platform that historical experience shows that when gold prices peak after a rapid rise, US stocks often follow suit. Currently, gold prices have risen to approximately 1.9 times their 20-quarter moving average, higher than the peak of approximately 1.7 times in 2008. If gold reverts to its long-term average, the S&P 500 index may face a correction of approximately 25%; a similar situation in 2008 triggered a drop of approximately 60%. Driven by factors such as the global energy crisis, both gold and stocks are at high levels. Even a mean reversion at this stage could exert downward pressure on the market.

        ▌US March CPI rose 3.3% year-on-year, a new high since May 2024

        April 10th news: The US March CPI rose 3.3% year-on-year, a new high since May 2024; the forecast was 3.4%, and the previous value was 2.4%. The seasonally adjusted US March CPI rose 0.9% month-on-month, in line with expectations, and the previous value was 0.30%.

        ▌US March Core CPI Rises 2.6% Year-on-Year, Below Forecast of 2.7%

        April 10th news report: The US core consumer price index rose 2.6% year-on-year in March, below the forecast of 2.7% and the previous value of 2.5%; it rose 0.2% month-on-month, below the forecast of 0.3%.

data.title: The TACO ceasefire merely put the Iran war on hold.
data.description:
Authors: Catherine Lucey, Courtney Subramanian, Eric Martin, and Jennifer A. Dlouhy, Bloomberg; Translator: Peggy, BlockBeats

From a "Stone Age" threat to a swift two-week ceasefire, the conflict surrounding Iran has undergone a dramatic shift in a short period. On the surface, the situation has cooled and markets have rebounded, but deeper structural problems have not been resolved.

On the one hand, Donald Trump, under political and economic pressure, chose to "step down," temporarily alleviating market and ally anxiety through a ceasefire; on the other hand, Iran has strengthened its control over the Strait of Hormuz during the conflict and is attempting to transform this geopolitical advantage into long-term leverage. The so-called "ceasefire" is more like a time-for-space arrangement than a true end to the problem.

Meanwhile, divisions within the United States are also intensifying. Significant cracks have emerged within the Republican Party regarding whether to cross the line into war and whether to accept an agreement that falls far short of its core objectives.

From the market's dramatic fluctuations to the constant shifts in policy statements, this crisis has not only exposed the highly personalized nature of decision-making mechanisms but has also prompted the world to reassess America's credibility and execution capabilities. With no clear victor, the conflict is entering a more complex phase: military deterrence, energy maneuvering, and political negotiations are intertwined. The real question is no longer "whether to cease fire," but how this game will be repriced, given that all parties' bottom lines remain firm. The following is the original text: US President Donald Trump has two weeks to determine whether he has untied the knot he himself tied on the Iran issue, or has instead tightened it further. His announcement on Tuesday of accepting a two-week ceasefire proposal with Iran brought relief to major capital cities around the world and jubilation to financial markets. At least for now, concerns have been temporarily set aside: that he will continue his attacks on civilian infrastructure and drag the region into deeper conflict through a bombing campaign he once described as "blowing Iran back to the Stone Age." But amidst this celebratory atmosphere of yet another Taco Tuesday, the president, known for his last-minute retreats, has once again teetered on the brink of disaster. A more realistic realization has emerged: none of the core issues have actually been resolved. Note: "Taco Tuesday" is a cultural phenomenon. In the United States, many restaurants offer discounts on tacos on Tuesdays, and over time, "Taco Tuesday" has become a lighthearted, celebratory, and even somewhat entertaining expression. One crucial unresolved issue is whether the Strait of Hormuz has truly restored passage for oil tankers. Despite some vague signals from Iran suggesting it would allow more ships to pass through the waterway, the reality remains unclear. Iran has shown little willingness to accept all of the US demands, whether it's weakening the existing regime or installing a pro-US leadership like Venezuela. Meanwhile, Tehran has not publicly committed to meeting Trump's two core demands: permanently abandoning its nuclear program and completely decommissioning its ballistic missile system. Moreover, the US president has previously threatened to "eradicate Iranian civilization," a statement that, if carried out, could cross the line into war crimes. On April 6, US President Donald Trump held a press conference at the White House. (Photo: Aaron Schwartz / CNP / Bloomberg)

Meanwhile, Trump confirmed that a "ten-point plan" proposed by Iran will serve as the basis for future negotiations. Tehran has previously demanded the lifting of sanctions and compensation for losses caused by the war. This could also mean imposing new fees on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, thus keeping shipping costs and energy prices high for an extended period.

From a practical perspective, for a US president under significant political and economic pressure and urgently needing to find a "way downhill," even if these goals cannot be fully achieved, he may have to accept it.

"It's not surprising that Trump clearly wants to postpone the issue again," said Ian Bremmer, founder of the Eurasia Group.

According to a senior government official who declined to be named, the White House deliberately created an atmosphere of suspense as Tuesday's 8 p.m. deadline approached. This was partly because the president himself instructed his advisors not to release any signals, leaving the outside world to speculate on his next move. At the same time, the US intensified its strikes against Iranian military targets, including its main oil export hub, Kharg Island, to increase its leverage in negotiations and demonstrate its military capabilities. According to a White House official, Vice President JD Vance played a central role in Tuesday's decision-making process, despite being in Hungary meeting with Viktor Orbán. At one point, Vance took Donald Trump on speakerphone at a rally in Orbán; the official also stated that the vice president had privately communicated directly with a Pakistani official acting as a mediator. The official added that Trump continued consultations with senior U.S. national security officials and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu throughout the day, and ultimately spoke with a Pakistani field marshal to confirm a ceasefire agreement. Meanwhile, the West Wing of the White House was abuzz with discussion over the release of a report that revealed widespread skepticism within the Trump administration prior to the outbreak of war regarding Israel's assessment that a military strike could incite a popular uprising in Iran and bring about a new secular government. The report also revealed Vance's initial opposition to the strike, as well as concerns expressed privately by several other senior aides. Against this backdrop, allies within Trump's political coalition spent much of Tuesday warning him against fulfilling his earlier extreme threat to "end Persian civilization." Conservative podcast hosts even openly discussed whether the cabinet should consider removing Donald Trump from office; even Republicans on Capitol Hill who have consistently supported him said his threats to crack down on power plants and desalination facilities had crossed the line. Although Trump's Republican Party won a special election in Georgia that day, the margin in this traditionally considered a "safe seat" narrowed significantly, seen as a potential sign of voter discontent. Meanwhile, economic pressures are mounting. Since the conflict erupted in late February, West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude oil prices have risen nearly 70%, pushing gas prices above $4 per gallon for the first time in years. Within an hour of Trump's announcement, oil prices fell 11%, highlighting the market's pent-up anxiety about his policy direction. The S&P 500 Index has fallen about 5.2% from its all-time high and just recorded its worst quarterly performance since 2022. Oil prices plummeted after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire. Brent crude fell as much as 16% on Wednesday. Trump's decision was made at the last minute. According to a US official, the president only fully heard Pakistan's proposal late Tuesday afternoon, about two hours after he announced the ceasefire on Truth Social. Whether to accept the proposal, and whether it met his own conditions, ultimately depended entirely on Donald Trump himself. Unknown Details Despite the president's claim in his statement that he had "moved this long-standing problem to near resolution," publicly available information offers little indication that this military and economic predicament, which has severely impacted his political standing, is moving towards genuine easing. Jennifer Kavanagh, director of military analysis at the libertarian think tank Defense Priorities, wrote on social media: "Trump's decision to back down tonight is certainly a relief. But if he was already prepared to back down, it was arguably the worst possible way. His previous escalation of the stakes has ultimately damaged his credibility and the perception of American power. This is a clear strategic failure." Within hours of Trump's ceasefire announcement, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt urged caution regarding reports of face-to-face talks, stating that any substantial progress must be confirmed by the president's official announcement. Meanwhile, according to Al Jazeera, Iran's proposals include demanding the withdrawal of US troops from their bases and deployments in the region and the unfreezing of Iranian assets. The likelihood of either the US or Israel accepting these conditions seems extremely slim. Although Washington has largely dismissed Iran's public statements throughout the conflict, believing Tehran is more flexible in private negotiations, this stark contrast between public and private statements highlights the deep-seated divisions and distrust that Trump must currently address. Jonathan Panikoff points out that a long-term solution requires addressing a series of core issues: the future of Iran's nuclear and missile programs, the fate of its highly enriched uranium stockpile, and the permanent guarantee of freedom of navigation. He stated, "With Iran willing to open the Strait of Hormuz, the president's acceptance of a two-week ceasefire certainly provided him with a 'stepping stone,' allowing him to declare a partial victory." Shortly after the ceasefire announcement, Trump, in an interview with AFP, stated that Iran's uranium stockpile would be "properly handled," without specifying the method, while still referring to the agreement as a "complete victory" for the United States. Energy and the "Energy Off-Ramp" Throughout his public career, Trump has consistently been less committed to aligning predetermined goals with reality; he has repeatedly paved the way for his "retreat" while key issues remain unresolved. He has also retreated from the most radical policies and threats on numerous occasions. For example, a year ago, after a sharp decline in financial markets, he quickly made concessions just days after implementing global tariffs. This administration may be pinning its hopes on: voters' short memories, the gradual recovery of the economy after the conflict's shock, and Iran's retaliation—something even Trump himself admitted was unexpected—which time can heal. However, if a deal close to the president's initial vision fails to materialize, the White House will inevitably face serious scrutiny. Jon Hoffman, a defense and foreign policy researcher at the Cato Institute, stated, "Overall, Trump's initial goals were extremely aggressive, including destroying Iran's nuclear program and even pushing for regime change, but neither of those has been achieved." Connecticut Democratic Senator Chris Murphy warned that if an agreement ultimately solidifies Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz, it will have "catastrophic consequences" for the world. In an interview with CNN, he stated, "It doesn't even sound like a real agreement right now because Trump's statements are completely inconsistent with Iran's. What a serious miscalculation it would be if Iran permanently controls the Strait, and how much of a failure this whole operation would appear." Trump's inconsistent rhetoric. Trump's public statements on the Iran war have repeatedly triggered sharp fluctuations in oil prices. For the United States, a rather ironic reality is that the five-week-long war may actually give Tehran more leverage in the global economy. During the conflict, Iran effectively strengthened its control over the Strait of Hormuz, virtually closing it to countries it considers "hostile," and is now pushing forward a plan to impose "passage fees" on oil tankers transiting the waterway. Clayton Seigle, a senior fellow at the Center for Strategic and International Studies in Washington, stated, "It is highly unlikely that Iran will relinquish its newly acquired, or at least strengthened, claim to control of the strait." Kevin Book, managing director of ClearView Energy Partners, headquartered in Washington, pointed out that the market views this ceasefire as a significant signal largely because it includes a commitment to reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

A naval vessel sails in the Strait of Hormuz on March 1, 2026. (Photo: Sahar Al Attar / AFP / Getty Images)

Meanwhile, the possibility of a significant drop in oil prices in the short term remains low.

Kevin Book, Managing Director of ClearView Energy Partners, said, "The lower end of the oil price range is still roughly around $100 per barrel." He added that if Donald Trump had actually carried out the threatened strike and triggered Iranian retaliation, the world could have faced an "unlimited" surge in oil prices.

GOP Offsides

Trump also needs to repair the divisions that have surfaced within his party on this issue. On Tuesday, a clear rift emerged within the Republican Party over whether to take a tougher stance against Iran.

Before the ceasefire, Trump warned that "tonight a whole civilization will be destroyed." In response, Texas Representative Nathaniel Moran, a Trump endorser, expressed concern on social media, saying that the United States is deviating from "the long-standing principles guiding America's national defense."

Wisconsin Republican Senator Ron Johnson stated that launching an attack on civilian infrastructure would be "a huge mistake." Wisconsin Senator Ron Johnson. (Photo: Brendan Smialowski / AFP / Getty Images) "If he attacks civilian targets, I can no longer support him. Whatever action we take must comply with the laws of war," Johnson told The Wall Street Journal. He also told podcast host John Solomon on Monday, "I hope, and I pray, that Trump is just bluffing. We are not at odds with the Iranian people; our goal is to 'liberate' them." Several former Trump allies, including Tucker Carlson, former Representative Marjorie Taylor Greene, and former White House official Anthony Scaramucci, have joined calls for his resignation, precisely because of his previous threats. After Trump shifted to a de-escalation stance, conservative opinion leaders Laura Loomer and Senator Lindsey Graham, who were previously key supporters of his hardline stance against Iran, began expressing concerns about the ceasefire agreement.

South Carolina Senator Lindsey Graham. (Photo: Anna Moneymaker / Getty Images)

“I don’t understand why anyone would treat this as a victory,” conservative opinion leader Laura Loomer wrote on social media. “Muslim communities, the so-called ‘Woke Reich,’ and the anti-Trump camp are using this ‘negotiation’ to attack President Trump while praising Tucker Carlson’s allies and calling for the invocation of the 25th Amendment against Trump.”

The Republican senator from South Carolina stated that Americans “must remember that after the outbreak of war, it was Iran that attacked the Strait of Hormuz, undermining freedom of navigation.”

The Republican senator from South Carolina stated that Americans “must remember that after the outbreak of war, it was Iran that attacked the Strait of Hormuz, violating freedom of navigation.”

"The crucial next step is to ensure that Iran does not gain anything from this hostile act against the world," Graham wrote on social media. He added that the United States must ensure control over "every bit" of highly enriched uranium within Iran. "Time will tell," the senator said.

data.title: Golden Morning Briefing | Iran reports progress in negotiations; BNB falls below $610
data.description:
Headline

▌Iran Reports on Negotiation Progress

On April 12, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Bagaei reported early this morning (April 12) on the progress of negotiations with the United States in Islamabad, Pakistan. According to his briefing, negotiations between Iran and the United States, mediated by Pakistan, began on the morning of April 11 and are still ongoing. The two sides have exchanged a great deal of information, and Iranian negotiators are “doing their utmost to protect Iran’s interests.” The briefing stated that the two sides discussed various aspects of the main negotiating issues, including the Strait of Hormuz, the nuclear issue, war reparations, lifting sanctions, and a complete end to the war against Iran and the Middle East. The briefing emphasized that the success of this diplomatic process depends on the sincerity of the United States, whether it makes excessive and illegal demands, and whether it accepts Iran's legitimate rights. The briefing also stated that Iran thanked Pakistan for its outstanding efforts in promoting and hosting these negotiations.

(CCTV International News) BNB has fallen below $610, currently trading at $609.13, a 24-hour increase of 0.44%. Market volatility is high; please manage your risk accordingly. **Market Updates** As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $72,855.04, up 1.10% in the last 24 hours; ETH price is $2,238.63, up 2.00% in the last 24 hours; BNB price is $604.72, up 0.2% in the last 24 hours; SOL price is $84.63, up 1.90% in the last 24 hours; DOGE price is $0.09354, up 1.10% in the last 24 hours. XRP price is $1.35, up 0.50% in the last 24 hours; TRX price is $0.3189, down 0.50% in the last 24 hours; WLFI price is $0.08105, down 3.90% in the last 24 hours; HYPE price is $41.82, up 5.00% in the last 24 hours.

Policy

▌Next Week, Lawmakers Will Return to Capitol Hill to Deal with Stablecoin Rewards

On April 12, negotiations on how to handle stablecoin rewards are intensifying ahead of lawmakers' return to Washington, D.C., with opinions being voiced from the White House, senior cabinet officials, lawmakers, and stakeholders from the cryptocurrency and banking sectors. Reaching an agreement would help advance a broader market structure bill aimed at regulating the cryptocurrency industry. This bill would clarify jurisdiction between the Securities and Exchange Commission and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, establish exchange rules, and require disclosure.

Policy

▌Next Week, Lawmakers Will Return to Capitol Hill to Deal with Stablecoin Rewards

▌The US government has transferred Bitcoin funds seized from Glenn Olivio to a Coinbase Prime address.

On April 12, according to data from Arkham Intelligence, the US government has transferred Bitcoin funds seized from Glenn Olivio to a Coinbase Prime address. In two separate transactions, two addresses, each labeled "US Government: Funds seized from Glenn Olivio," transferred 2.438 BTC (worth approximately $177,000) to the same Coinbase address, which begins with 3EMqu.

▌The US government has transferred Bitcoin funds seized from Glenn Olivio to a Coinbase Prime address.

▌US Comptroller of the Currency: The US Should Not Hand Over Digital Asset Innovation to Other Countries Due to Regulatory Uncertainty

On April 12, the US Comptroller of the Currency stated that the US should not hand over digital asset innovation to other countries due to regulatory uncertainty. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessenter emphasized that clarity is key to maintaining domestic markets, businesses, capital, and technology. A clear framework is how we ensure the US maintains its dominance and advantage in financial innovation, in line with Trump's vision of US leadership in the global economy.

...

▌The European Central Bank supports the EU's plan to transfer cryptocurrency regulation to the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA).

According to Reuters, the European Central Bank has formally approved the European Commission's plan to transfer the regulatory authority for major cryptocurrency companies, exchanges, and clearinghouses from national regulators to the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA). The European Central Bank warned that the ESMA must have adequate staffing and funding and recommended a phased transition to avoid disruption.

Blockchain Applications

▌Morgan Stanley: More Than Just Bitcoin, Exploring Tokenization and Tax Solutions to Advance Crypto Business

On April 12, Morgan Stanley's Amy Oldenburg stated that the Wall Street giant's exploration of its crypto business is still a long road, and it will focus on areas such as tokenization and tax solutions in the future.

Aave V4's deposit limit has been increased due to some assets reaching maximum capacity, in order to accommodate more liquidity. Aave stated in an X post that due to some assets reaching maximum capacity, Aave V4's deposit limit has been increased to accommodate more liquidity. As funds become more abundant, supply and lending limits will continue to gradually increase. Nebius is in talks to acquire Israeli startup AI21 Labs. On April 12th, Nebius was in talks to acquire Israeli startup AI21 Labs, a deal previously considered by Nvidia with a bid as high as $3 billion. The potential transaction would help Nebius expand its artificial intelligence services beyond server leasing, providing more services to its customers. Cryptocurrency ▌Ether Machine and Dynamix Terminate SPAC Merger Plans On April 12, Ether Machine's plan to go public through Dynamix Corporation ended. The institution, which originally planned to become an Ethereum asset management company, said in a statement released Friday evening that due to unfavorable market conditions, both parties had "mutually agreed to terminate" the previously announced business merger agreement with the Nasdaq-listed special purpose acquisition company (SPAC), "effective immediately." ▌Project Eleven CEO: Quantum Computing is Undergoing a "Paradigm Shift," and the Cryptocurrency Industry Cannot Ignore It On April 12, Project Eleven CEO @apruden08 stated that quantum computing is undergoing a "paradigm shift," and the cryptocurrency industry cannot ignore this shift. **Important Economic Developments** **▌US March CPI Rises to 3.3%, Slightly Below Expectations; Market Already Based; Bitcoin Tests 75,000 Resistance Again** On April 12th, US March inflation data was released. The US Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that the Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose 3.3% year-on-year, slightly lower than the market expectation of 3.4%; the core CPI rose 2.6% year-on-year, also lower than the expected 2.7%. Although the data was "more moderate than expected," the inflation level is still the highest since May 2024. Market reaction shows that the inflation trend had already been priced in. Interest rate expectations were quickly readjusted, and the timetable for interest rate cuts was pushed back to even further into 2026. In terms of risk assets, Bitcoin (BTC) rose 1.63% on the day, once again challenging the long-standing resistance level of $75,000, indicating that risk appetite has not weakened significantly despite geopolitical pressures. From a macro perspective, the rise in inflation is not sudden. Escalating tensions in the Middle East in early March triggered a supply shock to the oil market, with oil prices briefly exceeding $112 per barrel, pushing inflation expectations upwards ahead of time due to energy costs. In other words, the "lower than expected" CPI reflects more the market's prior priced-in inflationary pressure from energy sources than an immediate reaction to new information.

▌US Vice President Vance Extends Stay in Pakistan

On April 12, local time, it was learned from Pakistani government sources that, at Pakistan's request, US Vice President Vance extended his stay in Islamabad.

(CCTV News)

Golden Encyclopedia

▌Can Bitcoin Still Be Called "Digital Gold" Under Geopolitical Crises?

Every geopolitical crisis causes gold prices to rise while Bitcoin prices plummet. After six tests, the claim of "digital gold" has never been confirmed by data. Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves. For investors, Bitcoin has an asymmetry: it falls with stocks but does not rise with stocks. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an oversupply of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeatable behavioral records (behavioral accumulation).

Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "useful asset in times of crisis," and it can indeed play a role when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries are reduced, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold, but rather a completely new "next-generation gold."

Generational shifts and the widespread application of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.

data.title: Make AI your second brain in 5 minutes
data.description:
Author: AI Edge; Translator: Peggy, BlockBeats

This article introduces a personal knowledge system built on Claude Code and Obsidian. Its core is no longer the traditional RAG model of "each query, temporary retrieval," but rather an attempt to allow AI to continuously build and maintain an evolving knowledge base (wiki).

Structurally, the system can be divided into three layers:
·First, the raw data layer, including unmodifiable input sources such as notes, articles, and transcribed content;
·Second, the structured knowledge base maintained by AI, which completes cross-referencing and relationship building during continuous updates;
·Third, the schema rules layer, used to standardize the organization of knowledge and the system's operational logic.

Around this structure, the system operates through three core operations: Ingest, continuously incorporating external information into the system; Query, enabling immediate access to knowledge; and Lint, used to check structural consistency and fix potential problems. Under this mechanism, knowledge no longer remains a one-off dialogue result, but gradually accumulates into reusable long-term assets through a cycle of "writing—organizing—reusing." The authors propose that this model gives knowledge a "compound interest"-like cumulative effect: on the one hand, it reduces the cognitive burden on individuals, and on the other hand, it improves the accuracy and contextual consistency of the model's output. However, the effective operation of this system also relies on a prerequisite—continuous input and maintenance. Without stable data injection and structural updates, this "second brain" will struggle to achieve a true cumulative effect, and its advantages will diminish. The following is the original text: Claude Code + Obsidian is the most powerful AI combination I've ever used. I've practically built an "AI second brain," incorporating all my thoughts, reading, writing, online research, and other content. This includes my business plans, all my published YouTube videos, articles, and everything else important to me. Claude Code + Obsidian has rapidly gained popularity across various platforms, and this is no accident. For me personally, this AI system has greatly reduced my cognitive burden, allowing me to focus my energy on what truly matters—both business and personal life.

My Claude Code + Obsidian System

This system may look a bit complicated, but it only takes 5 minutes to set up. More importantly, it has a built-in memory mechanism and will continuously optimize itself as you use it.

Next, I will guide you step by step to replicate this "AI second brain" system, which can truly improve your efficiency.

I suggest you read to the end of the article—I will include a complete Claude Code + Obsidian operation cheat sheet, as well as all the resources mentioned in the article (all free).

I suggest you read to the end of the article—I will include a complete Claude Code + Obsidian operation cheat sheet, as well as all the resources mentioned in the article (all free).

3. Setting up Claude Code

Next, you need to configure a way to access Claude Code. For me (and probably for most people), the easiest way is to use the desktop client directly.

In the main chat interface, click "Select Folder," then find the Obsidian Vault you just created and select it.

Claude Code: Connect Your Vault

4. Set System Prompt

After you have selected the folder, the next step is to paste Andrej Karpathy's system prompt into the main chat box.

Your input should look like this:

Claude Code Initial Input

Building Your Database

You can think of Obsidian as a "blank notebook"—you need to actively input content at first, and the database will gradually be built up. Importable content includes: notes, CSV files, Markdown/text files, etc.

My Database

That's it! Your "AI Second Brain" is now built and ready to run. Next, I'll share some advanced tips to help you use it more efficiently.

Advanced Tips

1. Obsidian Chrome Extension

If you want to add data to your system more easily, simply install the Obsidian Chrome extension. It allows you to directly click "Add to Obsidian" while browsing the web to save content into your knowledge base with one click.

This will make building your "second brain" incredibly easy. I myself frequently use this feature to collect articles, web page data, research materials, and more. Example: Using the Obsidian Chrome extension. Note that data added through the extension is initially just an "isolated data source." Next, you can tell Claude Code, "I just added [x] to Obsidian; please integrate this into my Wiki." Claude Code will automatically link this new data to existing content, truly integrating it into your "second brain." This is also why this toolkit is so powerful. 2. Create separate folders (Vault) Andrej Karpathy recommends using two separate folders (Vault): • One for work/business content • One for personal life/goal management My own experience also confirms that this structure is the clearest and most effective. 3. Practicality I've found that the most valuable use of this system is actually quite simple: making your LLM suggestions more accurate. When the model has access to your complete personal information, business plan, writing background, and other contextual information, it can generate more "customized" and realistic high-quality prompts (even "super prompts"). Of course, this system has many more uses than just these, but if you only want to start with one of the most practical scenarios, I strongly recommend starting with "improving prompt quality." 4. Orphans (Orphaned Nodes) In Obsidian, "Orphans" refer to data points that are not connected to other notes. This feature is very useful because it can help you: • Find ideas that haven't been integrated yet. • Discover "weak areas" in the database. • Determine which content is worth further expansion or deepening. In other words, it's not just an organization tool, but also a mechanism to help you discover blind spots in your thinking. You can click the "three dots" in the upper right corner to find and turn on the Orphans switch to see which content hasn't been linked yet. Potential Disadvantages of This System We've already discussed many advantages, use cases, and optimization methods. So what are its shortcomings? In what situations might you be unsuitable for this system? 1. People Unaccustomed to Visualization A core advantage of this system is its ability to visualize data. If you don't rely on or are not comfortable with this method, its help may be limited. 2. Requires a Certain Maintenance Cost If you are unwilling to continuously maintain a database, this system may not be suitable for you. Although the maintenance cost is not high, it's difficult to realize its value without continuously inputting data into this "second brain." 3. Storage Consumption All content is stored locally as Markdown files, which will consume some device space. This is something that needs to be considered in advance.

data.title: What is the key to competition among crypto banks?
data.description:
Author: Pink Brains; Translator: Jia Huan, ChainCatcher

Market Landscape

Ask ten cryptocurrency users what digital banking is, and you'll likely get the same answer: a card that lets you spend stablecoins. Ask ten developers, and the answers will quickly diverge. Some are developing non-custodial wallets with Visa payment capabilities; others are forking Aave and calling it a savings account; and a few are seeking full banking licenses.

Monthly transaction volume for cryptocurrency cards grew from approximately $100 million in early 2023 to over $1.5 billion by the end of 2025 (a CAGR of 106%). The market is now worth over $18 billion annually. In 2025, stablecoin-linked bank card spending reached $4.5 billion, a year-on-year increase of 673%.

But let's look at who's handling these transaction volumes. On-chain bank card data shows that RedotPay, an Asia-based custody platform, dominates 60% of the market share, with transaction volume approximately four times that of its next 13 competitors combined. In contrast, DeFi-native, self-custodied digital banks pale in comparison on the transaction volume charts.

... However, the deeper story is the convergence of these crypto-friendly giants. Between December 2025 and March 2026: Coinbase applied for a National Trust License; NuBank received conditional OCC (Office of the Comptroller of the Currency) approval from the U.S. National Bank; PayPal applied to create PayPal Bank; Revolut obtained a full UK banking license and is pursuing a U.S. license; Kraken became the first crypto company to have a master account with the Federal Reserve; 11 companies applied for OCC approval within 83 days. Trust banking licenses include: Circle, Ripple, BitGo, Paxos, Fidelity, Bridge, Crypto.com, Morgan Stanley, Payoneer, Zerohash, and Protego. Over 50 crypto digital banks have already launched. The global digital banking market is projected to reach $552 billion by 2026 (according to The Business Research Company). We are trying to map the digital banking landscape—not just who is developing what, but who has the viable economic model. Core Conflicts: Two central conflicts shape the competitive landscape of digital banking. The first conflict is economic. 76% of traditional digital banks are unprofitable. Those that succeeded (Nubank, Revolut, SoFi) didn't profit from credit card spending, but from loan ledgers and net interest income. Fees were just the entry point; lending was the real core business. Now, crypto banks are joining the competition with fees and cash back—the very revenue model that led to the failure of the first generation of fintech companies. Stablecoins make matters worse: they squeeze foreign exchange margins to near zero. The second conflict concerns user choice. The crypto community praises self-custody, DeFi yields, and non-custodial wallets. But on-chain bank card transaction volume tells a different story. The vast majority of crypto card spending flows through custodial platforms, not because users are unfamiliar with self-custody, but because when you just want to buy a cup of coffee, the frictionless user experience outweighs the pursuit of control over your funds. History often follows a pattern: just as webmail predates encrypted email, Dropbox precedes self-hosted storage, and custodial exchanges dominate the market before DeFi—whether crypto banks will follow the same trajectory remains an open question. The Four Prototypes of Digital Banks Rather than using "Web2 vs. Web3" as a categorization (which only reflects technology and offers no insight into business models), a more useful perspective is to examine a digital bank's moat, unit economics, and its ceiling.

I. Encryption-Friendly & Banking Priority

The core profit point of digital banks lies in credit revenue and traffic conversion, rather than simply acting as a payment channel.

Nubank reported $15.8 billion in revenue in fiscal year 2025, with 85% coming from interest income. Credit card interest contributed $4.6 billion, and loan interest contributed $4.8 billion. Monthly revenue per active user reached $15, while the service cost was only $0.80, which is a 19-fold return.

SoFi obtained its banking license in 2022, and its quarterly net interest income grew from $94.9 million to $617 million in four years. Its deposit costs were 181 basis points lower than warehouse funding costs, resulting in annualized savings of approximately $680 million. Revolut achieved £3.1 billion in revenue across five business streams in 2024, with no single business line accounting for more than 30% of its revenue. Its trading/wealth business line saw a year-on-year growth of 298%. Licensed digital banks deliberately limit stablecoins to the payments sector because the loan ledger is the source of their economic benefits. Revolut has not yet introduced stablecoin balance yields; its participation in the UK FCA sandbox test in February 2026 involved using its proprietary stablecoin as a payment infrastructure, not a savings product. SoFi's stablecoin (SoFiUSD, launched in December 2025) operates through a settlement channel via the Mastercard network. This restraint is built on a specific market condition: on-chain yields are currently uncompetitive. Aave v3's USDC yield is currently 2.6% APY, lower than SoFi's 3.3% savings APY and Revolut Ultra's 4.25%. However, on-chain yields are subject to cyclical compression. During periods of high DeFi activity, Aave USDC reached 8-10%, and Ethena's yield, driven by funding rates, was significantly higher. This gap is cyclical, and when it widens again, the competitive dynamics will change.

II. Commercial and Social Super Apps

MercadoPago, Grab, WeChat, Alipay. They didn't initially intend to build banks, but rather embedded finance into their commercial applications. Their moat isn't the product itself, but rather their distribution channels and behavioral data—which allows them to conduct credit assessments more accurately than any bank.

MercadoPago's credit revenue grew from $246 million in 2020 to $5.9 billion in 2025, a 24-fold increase in five years. Grab's loan portfolio grew from $185 million in 2022 to $1.3 billion by the end of 2025, with financial services revenue reaching $348 million in fiscal year 2025.

Both have explored stablecoins. MercadoPago launched Meli Dólar (MUSD) in Brazil and expanded to Chile and Mexico, but MUSD has a circulating market capitalization of only $65 million, less than 0.4% of its $19 billion AUM. Grab partnered with StraitsX for stablecoin settlements, allowing tourists to instantly settle in Singapore dollars using the XSGD stablecoin at GrabPay merchants in Singapore. Neither has explored stablecoin yields. This is the breakthrough for crypto natives and the most undervalued gap in this space. Whop is worth watching here. It's not currently a digital bank, but a creator marketplace. But after Tether's $200 million investment (valuing the company at $1.6 billion), creators can accept USDT, hold stablecoins, and settle transactions without banks. Plasma's integration with Aave offers stablecoin yields, targeting 18.4 million users and $3 billion in annual creator revenue. MercadoPago in 2003 wasn't a digital bank either, but rather a third-party guarantor for the market—financial relationships were inherent to commerce. Whop is currently at that same starting point, built on a crypto network from day one. The implication for card-centric digital banks is that the most enduring financial relationships may not begin with finance at all, but with e-commerce. III. Transaction Priority Robinhood, Coinbase, Binance, Kraken, Bybit, OKX—starting with centralized cryptocurrency exchanges and expanding into crypto banking. Each platform in this group is explicitly building a banking layer to generate revenue that doesn't rely on bull markets. Robinhood is the most complete example: its total assets grew by approximately 70% year-over-year to $324 billion, and net deposits reached a record $68 billion. Coinbase is aggressively expanding into digital banking: its own L2 blockchain (Base), a wallet with card-swiping capabilities, crypto-enabled lending based on Morpho, Bitcoin-backed loans in partnership with Better, and a pending trust license application. Kraken holds both a trust license and a Federal Reserve master account. These platforms started with transaction revenue that already had economies of scale and are now adding banking services on top of that. Stablecoin digital banks take the opposite approach: starting with meager fees and hoping to build everything on top of that—which is far more difficult.

IV. Stablecoin-First (Crypto-Native)

Ether.fi, Gnosis Pay, RedotPay, KAST, Holyheld, Bleap, Ready, Tria, Cypher, Payy, and dozens of others. These platforms leverage the low operating costs of stablecoins and the composability of DeFi as a backend product infrastructure. Their value proposition is clear: self-custody; DeFi yields (5-15% APY in active markets, compared to 3-4% for traditional savings); near-instantaneous cross-border payments based on stablecoins with extremely low foreign exchange fees; and global portability without geographical limitations.

Stablecoin-first digital banks have the most significant structural advantages in emerging markets and cross-border use cases.

But the weaknesses are equally real: none have made a breakthrough in scaling unsecured loans; they compete on the thinnest profit margin (fees) while subsidizing user acquisition with token-backed cash rebates; stablecoins make matters worse—compressing foreign exchange profits and settlement fees to near zero, eroding the revenue streams that sustain early digital banks. Most crypto digital banks are simply built on the front end of a shared infrastructure. Understanding this technology stack is crucial for assessing their competitive advantages.

Bank Card Networks (Visa, Mastercard): Although nearly equal in the number of projects (over 130 each), Visa accounts for over 90% of on-chain bank card transaction volume through early partnerships with crypto-native infrastructure providers. This is the single point of failure risk for the entire industry—if Visa changes its crypto project policies, slows its expansion, or raises fees, the economics of the entire industry could change overnight.

Issuing Institutions (Rain, Reap, Baanx, StraitsX): Regulated bridges between the on-chain world and traditional finance. The most important structural development is the emergence of full-stack issuing institutions—these companies directly hold prime memberships with Visa/Mastercard, bypassing traditional sponsoring banks.

Issuing Institutions (Rain, Reap, Baanx, StraitsX): Regulated bridges between the on-chain world and traditional finance. The most important structural development is the emergence of full-stack issuing institutions—companies that directly hold prime memberships with Visa/Mastercard, bypassing traditional sponsoring banks.

Most crypto banks share the same backend. Rain powers Ether.fi, RedotPay, and Avalanche Card. If Rain experiences technical glitches, regulatory issues, or a strategic shift, the entire industry will be impacted. A report by Solus Partners analyzing 19 platforms points to infrastructure concentration and vendor dependence as systemic risks—the Synapse risk for crypto banks. (Note: Synapse was a US fintech infrastructure company that filed for bankruptcy in 2024 due to a funding segregation gap, resulting in the freezing of funds for users of dozens of partner platforms that relied on its services, becoming a landmark warning case for the industry regarding the risks of infrastructure concentration.) The Threat of Wallet-Native Stablecoins A frequently overlooked competitive dynamic: major wallet providers are issuing their own stablecoins specifically to fund debit card spending, building closed-loop ecosystems to capture value that would otherwise be earned by independent digital banks. In late Q3 2025, MetaMask launched mUSD and Phantom launched CASH, both designed as funding mechanisms for their respective debit card products. These wallets do not rely on users holding USDC or USDT, but rather create a closed loop—users convert their assets into the wallet's native stablecoin, which they then use to make card payments. Early data showed drastically different trajectories: Phantom's CASH steadily grew from approximately $25 million in September to approximately $100 million in late December; MetaMask's mUSD, after peaking at nearly $100 million in early October, fell to approximately $25 million, a 75% decrease. If a wallet successfully makes its native stablecoin the default source of funds, transaction fees, forex spreads, and reserve earnings are retained by the wallet platform itself. MetaMask, Phantom, and Coinbase Wallet all have established user relationships—adding digital banking functionality is an extension of their product lines, not a new product. Independent crypto banks may therefore lose a significant portion of their value proposition. The Economic Dilemma: 76% of traditional digital banks are unprofitable. Crypto-native digital banks inherit the same broken model—and stablecoins make things worse, not better. The lesson from bank-first players is clear: payments are merely a distribution channel, not a core business. Nubank's 85% interest income share proves this; the expansion of net interest margin (NIM) driven by SoFi licenses proves it. Crypto banks that use bank card spending as their core revenue engine are building on sand. A sustainable model uses bank cards as a channel to acquire users while monetizing them through higher-margin on-chain finance: DeFi yields, exchange trading, structured products, and lending. Five Game-Changing Things 1. On-Chain Credit Scoring In the crypto world, wallet transaction history, DeFi usage patterns, repayment behavior on lending protocols, staking duration, transaction frequency, and protocol diversity can all serve as inputs for credit assessment. Currently, no crypto bank has achieved this at scale. Whoever cracks this first will essentially be replicating Nubank's approach on permissionless on-chain infrastructure. 2. Crypto-Native Enterprises Obtaining Full Banking Licenses Not trust licenses (custody only), but full licenses that allow for deposit taking and lending. This will allow crypto-native enterprises to build loan ledgers using stablecoin deposits as funding sources at a lower cost than warehouse financing facilities. 3. Regulatory clarification on the legality of returns. The regulatory direction in major markets is consistent: stablecoin issuers are not allowed to pay returns. The United States and Europe have clearly drawn this red line, and major Asian markets such as Japan, Singapore, and Hong Kong have also adopted similar conservative stances.

4. Agent-Driven Finance

AI agents perform financial operations on behalf of users—rebalancing portfolios, optimizing returns, managing payments, and executing cross-protocol strategies. Mastercard had 6 crypto partners in 2024, expanding to over 25 by 2025. Visa launched Smart Commerce Connect, enabling AI agents to shop on behalf of users at merchants worldwide.

Whoever can build the best proxy infrastructure on top of stablecoin networks will capture the next wave of traffic in the e-commerce sector. 5. Making On-Chain Operations Seamless Cryptocurrency banks still rely on bank card networks, but payment terminal technologies that bypass them already exist: QR code payments settled in stablecoins, NFC touch payments without Apple Pay or Google Pay, and physical card swipes settled on-chain. Projects like OpenPasskey (based on Base) are proving this path is feasible: possessing its own ISO-issued IIN, P-256 cryptography, and fully non-custodial encrypted cards—three payment methods, no Visa required, no bank needed. Who will win? We don't know yet. But the key variables for victory are already there. Licensed digital banks have proven their economic model and hold an advantage in credit-driven user relationships, which is the case in most developed markets. Stablecoin-first digital banks offer globally portable USD, local stablecoins in emerging markets, access to DeFi yields, and retrospective rewards, but current data shows users still prefer simplicity and ease of use over crypto-native concepts. Business-embedded players may possess the deepest moats because they already control distribution channels—but layering crypto functionality on top of mature infrastructure is costly, requires user education, and is highly dependent on regulatory clarity. The value captured by the infrastructure layer (card issuers, custody, fiat deposits and withdrawals, core banking systems, blockchain settlement, KYC/AML) is destined to surpass that of any consumer brand. Over 40 stablecoin cards are in a race to the bottom with token-subsidized cash back offers, lacking true business moats and sharing the same infrastructure—most of which won't survive the next two years. The landscape of encrypted digital banking is at a turning point.

data.title: Entrepreneurs in the AI ​​Era Are Being Redefined – Observations from Xiaohongshu Hackathon
data.description:
Author: Lawyer Shao Shiwei

I. Lowering the Technical Barrier is a Good Thing

Let me first mention a statistic that impressed me deeply. This year, at the Claude Code Hackathon hosted by Anthropic, out of 13,000 applicants, only one of the top five had a design engineer background; the rest were lawyers, doctors, infrastructure professionals, and other industry practitioners.

This would have been unimaginable five years ago. Five years ago, if you wanted to create a software product, the first thing you had to do was hire a technical partner because you couldn't get past the coding hurdle. Now, AI helps you pass most of that coding hurdle.

What does this mean?

The competition format of Xiaohongshu was quite interesting—48 hours of closed development. When time is pushed to the limit, there's no time to show off skills. You can only choose the problem you're most familiar with, the one that hurts the most, and the one you most want to solve.

The most touching projects at the event all conformed to this rule.

Brain-Controlled Wheelchair won first prize in the hardware track. The developer himself is a wheelchair user who has experienced two severe paralysis episodes. The device controls the wheelchair through a mixture of EEG and EMG signals, and he designed the headband to resemble the Golden Cudgel. A person trapped inside their own body created their own Golden Cudgel. This is a pain-driven product.

The device uses a hybrid control technology combining brainwaves and electromyography (EMG). Users wear a headband and, by concentrating their thoughts and coordinating with subtle head muscle movements, can control the wheelchair's forward, backward, and turning motions. To make the operation extremely easy, the complex control logic was greatly simplified, retaining only the most core movements. His wife, "Xianggu," is the team's "brainstorming expert." Originally an online novelist, she transformed herself into a geek developer for this project. Two people, a wheelchair, a magic staff, 48 hours. On stage, Fei Niu said that he wanted to create more than just a product; he wanted to offer a new possibility to those trapped in their bodies—allowing them to reconnect with the world using the faintest signals, supporting their gradual recovery. Sitting in the audience listening to this, I realized that behind every most moving product in this competition stood a developer who was "the user themselves." In addition, there are some other interesting products. Mind Kit addresses the problem of interrupted train of thought during deep conversations with AI—it breaks down the dialogue into independent thought nodes, allowing AI to process multiple tasks in parallel, ultimately condensing them into a thought topology map. The developers themselves had enough of this, so their creations are incisive and to the point. There's also a team of middle school students with an average age of 13 who are developing a tool to help creators build viral Xiaohongshu (Little Red Book) posts using AI. They spend their days browsing Xiaohongshu and figuring out how to gain followers, so their understanding of this scenario is deeper than most adult professionals. Judging from the feedback from the audience, the popular products didn't start from "what AI can do," but rather from "what I (or those around me) are struggling with." The 48-hour competition format simply amplified this trend. It forced developers to skip over all the "cool-sounding" pseudo-needs and strive to find "real problems." Xiaohongshu is the most suitable stage for this transformation. It deserves special mention. Its uniqueness doesn't lie in its large user base. Its uniqueness lies in consolidating five things previously scattered across GitHub, Twitter, search engines, and e-commerce platforms—identifying pain points, publicizing, providing real-time feedback, precise search, and closed-loop conversion—into a single arena. The traditional product development path was: have an idea → raise funds → hire staff → develop → launch → find users. Each step had high barriers to entry and required substantial capital investment. The current path is becoming: have an idea → AI-assisted development → post on Xiaohongshu (a Chinese social media platform) → collect feedback → decide whether to scale up. The steps cut in between were among the most expensive in the past decade. This means the cost of trial and error in entrepreneurship has been reduced to a level that ordinary people can afford. The cost of failure has changed from "losing all savings" to "a weekend." This is what makes the AI ​​era truly exciting—it doesn't allow a few geniuses to create something even more amazing; it allows most ordinary people to **participate** for the first time.

IV. Some Advice for Entrepreneurs in the AI ​​Era

After reading these past two days, if I were to give some specific advice to entrepreneurs who are on their way or preparing to embark on their journey, I would say these things.

First, stop asking "What can AI do?" and ask "What's bothering me lately?" All products that start with technology and work backward to find application scenarios will lose to products that start with real pain points and work backward to find technical solutions. Your advantage is not AI—everyone has AI—your advantage is that you understand the specific pain points in a particular scenario better than others.

Second, cross-industry experience is not a disadvantage, it's an asset. If you are a lawyer, doctor, teacher, nurse, decorator, or used car dealer, please don't think that "not understanding technology" is a problem. You have something more than people with a purely technical background: "industry experience." This is something AI can't learn, and neither can engineers; it can only be learned through years of experience in that industry. Third, release it first, then scale it up. Don't go down the old path of "working behind closed doors for a year before launching." You can have an idea today, create it tomorrow, and release it the day after to see feedback. Let the market tell you if it's worth doing, not your own self-pity. Fourth, warmth is the last line of defense. The most touching products at the event all had one thing in common—they made people feel that "the person who made this genuinely wanted to help someone else." This was true for the mind-controlled wheelchair, the middle school student's note-taking tool, and every project that caught the judges' attention. AI can help you write code, create PPTs, and generate content, but it can't create empathy for you. When everyone uses the same tools, your depth of understanding of people is your only differentiator. Fifth, execution is paramount. None of the winners in this competition had the most "outlandish ideas"; they were all people who "actually got things running within 48 hours." The biggest illusion of the AI ​​era is that because the barrier to entry is low, everyone feels they can "start anytime." But the truth is: things that can be started anytime, most people will never start in their entire lives As a lawyer who has long handled cases in the new economy and Web3 fields, the reason I came to Zhangjiang to watch the competition these past two days is because my client base—entrepreneurs in the AI ​​and Web3 fields—is the protagonist of this transformation. These past two days, I've witnessed a group of incredibly intelligent, passionate individuals who, for the first time, have truly mastered the tools of creation. Many of them will create things we can't even imagine today in the coming years. However, from the specific cases I've handled, I've also seen the other side of the coin: The risks on the entrepreneurial path rarely arrive suddenly at a single point in time; they often quietly grow from the daily, concrete decisions. The design of a product feature, the wording of a promotional piece, an adjustment to a business model, the selection of a partner—each item seems small on its own, so small that you wouldn't even stop to think about it at the moment. But one day, they can converge into a situation you never anticipated. The more cases I handle over the years, the more I feel that a lawyer shouldn't just be someone who "cleans up the mess after things go wrong." In this era of AI, where iterations happen on a weekly basis, what entrepreneurs truly need is someone who walks alongside them, someone who can point out the pitfalls before they even see them—someone to accompany them on the long journey, not just someone waiting for them at the finish line.

data.title: The long-awaited V5 – Uniswap faces an innovation dilemma.
data.description:
Author: Gu Yu, ChainCatcher

In the crypto industry, the speed of innovation is often a crucial measure of a protocol's longevity. However, Uniswap, which once single-handedly ushered in the DeFi Summer, seems to be slowing down.

Looking back at Uniswap's evolutionary history, its iteration pace has always been renowned for its rapid pace: V1, featuring an AMM mechanism, was born in 2018; V2 introduced ERC-20/ERC-20 pairs in 2020; V3 launched centralized liquidity in 2021; and V4 introduced programmable Hooks in 2023. On average, every 1.5 years, Uniswap introduces a highly innovative mechanism, reshaping the flow path of hundreds of billions of dollars.

However, time has frozen in April 2026, nearly three years since the announcement of the V4 solution, and the market still hasn't seen any updates on the next version of Uniswap. Currently, Uniswap's product development still revolves around Uniswap V4, which transforms Uniswap from a fixed-function AMM into a highly programmable liquidity platform. The core innovation of this version is Hooks, a modular, programmable plugin that allows developers to insert custom logic throughout the pool's lifecycle, such as implementing dynamic fees, limit orders, TWAMM (Time-Weighted Market Maker), MEV protection, loss hedging, custom oracles, Continuous Clearing Auctions (CCA), and virtually limitless other functionalities. In other words, previously many developers needed to fork Uniswap to implement certain functions, leading to the diversion of Uniswap liquidity among various forked DEXs. Now, developers only need to write a Hook contract to significantly enhance the customization of Uniswap liquidity pools. Although Uniswap announced the specific concept for version V4 in June 2023, for security reasons, this version underwent nine independent audits and a large-scale security competition, causing its mainnet launch to be delayed from the initial Q3 2024 to the end of January 2025. After launch, the total value locked in Uniswap V4 peaked at over $1.2 billion, currently falling back to $650 million, but still only 40% of version V3 and lagging behind version V2. Meanwhile, Uniswap is also steadily expanding its use cases and markets. In the past six months, Uniswap has launched blockchains such as Linea, Tempo, X Layer, and Monad to gain a competitive edge in emerging blockchains. Simultaneously, Uniswap is actively expanding its API partnerships, with recent announcements including Anchorage Digital, Ledger, Privy, and MetaMask. Of course, Uniswap has not been inactive in product development in recent years; for example, the application chain Uniswap and the CCA token auction product have received significant market response. In February 2025, Uniswap's application chain, Unichain, officially launched its mainnet, attracting integration from over 90 DeFi protocols. Its total value transferred (TVL) plummeted from a peak of $900 million to $36 million. At the end of 2025, Uniswap announced the launch of its Continuous Liquidation Auction (CCA) feature, specifically designed for price discovery and liquidity launches for new assets, and subsequently partnered with Aztec and Rainbow to complete token sales. However, it must be pointed out that at this point in 2025, application chains and token auctions were very "outdated" business models in the crypto space. Many projects with existing core businesses facing bottlenecks were exploring "chain launches" and Launchpad-like services. Uniswap also began to follow suit, but its actual impact was very limited. In earlier years, Uniswap explored the NFT trading market and on-chain games by acquiring Genie and Crypto: The Game for substantial sums. Subsequent data revealed these to be two failed acquisitions. In the past year, Uniswap has seen the departures of several key figures: Head of Growth Sarina Siddhanti, Chief Operating Officer Mary Catherine Lader, Head of Strategy and Operations Zach Wong, Chief Legal Officer Katherine Minarik, and Head of Venture Capital Julia Rosenberg. This reflects Uniswap's struggles in controlling operating costs and its sluggish growth. Uniswap's overall decline is also reflected in the price of its governance token, UNI. In the past year, UNI's price has fallen by more than 74%, dropping below $2 at its lowest point, significantly exceeding the overall decline of major cryptocurrencies and prompting market analysts to criticize UNI as having "no real value." In September 2025, Arca's Chief Investment Officer, Jeff Dorman, responded to Uniswap founder Hayden Adams' tweet regarding the protocol's performance, stating, "We are not bearish on Uniswap, but we are bearish on UNI. In today's market and changing regulatory environment, it's just a completely meaningless token. Your and your venture capitalists' positions don't matter; either switch to a revenue-sharing or buyback model, or don't make a token at all." In response to market skepticism and to give UNI more value, the Uniswap Foundation proposed in November of last year to enable a protocol fee switch for Uniswap V2 and V3 versions for buying back and burning UNI, while also burning 100 million UNI from the vault. Subsequently, UNI surged nearly 38% in a short period, peaking at over $9. According to DeFillama data, Uniswap's recent daily transaction fee revenue has been between $100,000 and $200,000, with a 30-day revenue of $3.93 million and an annualized revenue of approximately $46 million. This still lags behind other DeFi protocols such as PancakeSwap, Jupiter, Lido, and Aave.
